description: 3 gotchas about SQLite WAL mode — should consolidate into one wisdom note

candidates.content: SQLite WAL mode requires shared memory access — fails on network-mounted filesystems (NFS, SMB). The -shm file must be on a local disk.
SQLite in WAL mode allows only a single writer at a time. Concurrent writes queue behind a lock, leading to SQLITE_BUSY if the timeout is too short.
SQLite WAL checkpoint can block readers if the WAL file grows too large. Set PRAGMA wal_autocheckpoint to prevent unbounded growth.
